notes: Figurative risk family. This branch models Gratwanderung as a delicate course between two failure modes, not literal mountain hiking.

de.definition: Ein heikles Vorgehen zwischen zwei Risiken, bei dem jeder Fehltritt schadet.
de.usage_note: Nur der übertragene Sinn, nicht das Wandern im Gebirge.
examples: Zwischen Offenheit und Diskretion bewegt sich ihre Rede auf schmalem Grat.

en.definition: A delicate course between two risks where any misstep causes harm.
examples: Her speech was a balancing act between openness and discretion.

it.definition: Un percorso delicato tra due rischi, dove ogni passo falso provoca danni.
examples: Il suo discorso è stato un equilibrismo tra apertura e discrezione.

notes: Prototypicality family. This branch models Inbegriff as the purest embodiment of a quality.

de.definition: Die reinste Verkörperung einer Eigenschaft oder Idee.
de.usage_note: Fast immer mit Genitiv: der reinste Ausdruck von etwas sein.
examples: Für viele ist dieses Haus die reinste Verkörperung von Luxus.

en.definition: The purest embodiment of a quality or idea.
examples: For many, this house is the epitome of luxury.

it.definition: L'incarnazione più pura di una qualità o di un'idea.
examples: Per molti questa casa è la quintessenza del lusso.

notes: Consequence-scope family, next to concept-b1-auswirkung. This branch models Tragweite as how far the consequences of something reach.

de.definition: Das Ausmaß, in dem sich die Folgen einer Sache auswirken.
de.usage_note: Typisch: die volle Bedeutung einer Entscheidung erst spät erkennen.
examples: Erst Jahre später wurde klar, wie weit die Folgen dieser Entscheidung reichten.

en.definition: How far the consequences of something reach.
examples: Only years later did the full ramifications of the decision become clear.

it.definition: La misura in cui si estendono le conseguenze di qualcosa.
it.usage_note: Qui solo nel senso figurato di ampiezza delle conseguenze, non la portata di un fiume o di un pranzo.
examples: Solo anni dopo divenne chiara la portata di quella decisione.

notes: Necessity family. This branch models unabdingbar as strictly necessary, with no possible waiver.

de.definition: So notwendig, dass darauf unter keinen Umständen verzichtet werden kann.
examples: Gegenseitiges Vertrauen ist für diese Zusammenarbeit absolut notwendig.

en.definition: So necessary that it cannot be given up under any circumstances.
examples: Mutual trust is indispensable for this collaboration.

it.definition: Così necessario che non se ne può fare a meno in nessuna circostanza.
examples: La fiducia reciproca è imprescindibile per questa collaborazione.

notes: Care-and-precision family. This branch models akribisch as extremely careful down to the smallest detail.

de.definition: Mit äußerster Genauigkeit bis ins kleinste Detail.
examples: Sie dokumentiert jeden einzelnen Schritt des Experiments bis ins kleinste Detail.

en.definition: Extremely careful, down to the smallest detail.
examples: She keeps meticulous records of every step of the experiment.

it.definition: Estremamente accurato, fino al più piccolo dettaglio.
examples: Tiene una documentazione meticolosa di ogni fase dell'esperimento.

notes: Logical-rigour family, next to concept-c1-stichhaltig. This branch models stringent as logically tight argumentation.

de.definition: Logisch lückenlos und zwingend aufgebaut.
de.usage_note: Für Argumentationen und Beweisführungen, nicht für strenge Regeln.
examples: Ihre Beweisführung ist lückenlos aufgebaut und lässt keine Einwände zu.

en.definition: Built with airtight, compelling logic.
examples: Her rigorous argument leaves no room for objections.

it.definition: Costruito con logica serrata e convincente.
examples: La sua argomentazione stringente non lascia spazio a obiezioni.

notes: Internal-consistency family, next to concept-c1-konsequent (which owns the IT label coerente). This branch models kohaerent as internally consistent and well connected.

de.definition: In sich stimmig und ohne innere Widersprüche verbunden.
examples: Die einzelnen Kapitel fügen sich zu einem stimmigen Gesamtbild.

en.definition: Internally consistent and well connected, without contradictions.
examples: The chapters form a coherent overall picture.

it.definition: Internamente consistente e ben collegato, senza contraddizioni.
examples: I capitoli formano un quadro complessivo coerente.

notes: Concise-expression family. This branch models praegnant as saying much in few, sharply chosen words.

de.definition: Kurz und treffend formuliert, mit viel Inhalt in wenigen Worten.
examples: Ihre Zusammenfassung bringt das ganze Projekt in zwei Sätzen auf den Punkt.

en.definition: Expressed briefly and sharply, packing much into few words.
examples: Her incisive summary captures the whole project in two sentences.

it.definition: Espresso in modo breve e penetrante, con molto contenuto in poche parole.
examples: La sua sintesi incisiva racchiude l'intero progetto in due frasi.

notes: Speaking-skill family. This branch models eloquent as fluent and persuasive in speech.

de.definition: Sprachlich gewandt und überzeugend im Ausdruck.
examples: Er antwortet auf jede kritische Frage flüssig und überzeugend.

en.definition: Fluent and persuasive in expression.
examples: He gave an eloquent answer to every critical question.

it.definition: Fluente e persuasivo nell'espressione.
examples: Ha dato una risposta eloquente a ogni domanda critica.

notes: Fine-distinction family, next to concept-b2-subtilitat. This branch models subtil as fine and hard to notice.

de.definition: Fein und kaum merklich, nur bei genauem Hinsehen erkennbar.
examples: Der Unterschied zwischen den beiden Fassungen ist fein und leicht zu übersehen.

en.definition: Fine and hard to notice, visible only on close inspection.
examples: The difference between the two versions is subtle and easy to miss.

it.definition: Fine e difficile da notare, visibile solo osservando con attenzione.
it.usage_note: Qui solo nel senso figurato di differenza o allusione appena percettibile, non di oggetto poco spesso.
examples: La differenza tra le due versioni è sottile e facile da non notare.

notes: Complexity family. This branch models vielschichtig as having many layers or aspects.

de.definition: Aus vielen Ebenen oder Aspekten zusammengesetzt.
examples: Das Problem hat mehr Ebenen, als es auf den ersten Blick scheint.

en.definition: Made up of many layers or aspects.
examples: The problem is more multilayered than it first appears.

it.definition: Composto da molti livelli o aspetti.
examples: Il problema è più sfaccettato di quanto sembri a prima vista.

notes: Contradiction family, next to concept-c1-implizieren cluster. This branch models widerspruechlich as containing statements that cannot all be true.

de.definition: So beschaffen, dass sich die enthaltenen Aussagen gegenseitig ausschließen.
examples: Die beiden Zeugen erzählen Versionen, die sich gegenseitig ausschließen.

en.definition: Containing statements that cannot all be true at once.
examples: The two witnesses gave contradictory accounts.

it.definition: Che contiene affermazioni che non possono essere tutte vere allo stesso tempo.
examples: I due testimoni hanno fornito versioni contraddittorie.

notes: Acceptance family. This branch models unumstritten as accepted by everyone, with no serious challenge.

de.definition: Von allen anerkannt und ohne ernsthaften Widerspruch.
examples: Ihre fachliche Autorität wird von niemandem ernsthaft in Frage gestellt.

en.definition: Accepted by everyone, without serious challenge.
examples: Her professional authority is undisputed.

it.definition: Riconosciuto da tutti, senza contestazioni serie.
examples: La sua autorità professionale è indiscussa.

notes: Impartiality family. This branch models befangen as unable to judge neutrally because of personal involvement, including the legal sense.

de.definition: Wegen persönlicher Beteiligung nicht in der Lage, neutral zu urteilen.
de.usage_note: Auch juristisch: ein Richter kann wegen Naehe zu einer Partei abgelehnt werden.
examples: Als enger Freund des Angeklagten darf er in diesem Fall nicht urteilen.

en.definition: Unable to judge neutrally because of personal involvement.
examples: As a close friend of the defendant, he is too biased to judge the case.

it.definition: Incapace di giudicare in modo neutrale a causa di un coinvolgimento personale.
examples: Da amico stretto dell'imputato, è troppo prevenuto per giudicare il caso.

notes: Retrospective-evaluation family. This branch models rueckblickend as judging something from a later point in time.

de.definition: Von einem späteren Zeitpunkt aus betrachtet und bewertet.
examples: Aus heutiger Sicht war der Umzug die beste Entscheidung ihres Lebens.

en.definition: Judged from a later point in time.
examples: In hindsight, the move was the best decision of her life.

it.definition: Giudicato da un momento successivo nel tempo.
examples: Col senno di poi, il trasloco è stata la decisione migliore della sua vita.

notes: Mismatch family, next to concept-a2-unterschied. This branch models Diskrepanz as a striking mismatch between two things that should align.

de.definition: Ein auffälliges Missverhältnis zwischen Dingen, die eigentlich zusammenpassen sollten.
examples: Zwischen Anspruch und Wirklichkeit klafft hier eine deutliche Lücke.

en.definition: A striking mismatch between things that should align.
examples: There is a clear discrepancy between the plan and the results.

it.definition: Un divario evidente tra cose che dovrebbero corrispondere.
examples: C'è una chiara discrepanza tra il piano e i risultati.

notes: Dilemma family, next to concept-b2-dilemma. This branch models Zwickmuehle as a bind where every available option has a serious drawback.

de.definition: Eine Lage, in der jede mögliche Entscheidung einen ernsten Nachteil hat.
examples: Egal wie sie sich entscheidet, eine Seite wird enttäuscht sein.

en.definition: A bind in which every available option has a serious drawback.
examples: She is in a quandary: whatever she decides, someone will be disappointed.

it.definition: Una situazione in cui ogni opzione disponibile comporta uno svantaggio serio.
examples: È in un'impasse: qualunque cosa decida, qualcuno resterà deluso.

notes: Inner-conflict family, next to concept-c2-ambivalent. This branch models Zwiespalt as being torn between two feelings or loyalties.

de.definition: Das Gefühl, zwischen zwei Empfindungen oder Loyalitäten hin- und hergerissen zu sein.
examples: Sie fühlt sich zwischen Familie und Karriere hin- und hergerissen.

en.definition: The feeling of being torn between two feelings or loyalties.
examples: She feels an inner conflict between family and career.

it.definition: La sensazione di essere combattuti tra due sentimenti o lealtà.
examples: Vive un dissidio interiore tra famiglia e carriera.

notes: Agreement family, next to concept-b1-vereinbarung and concept-b2-einigung. This branch models Konsens as broad shared agreement within a group, not a negotiated deal.

de.definition: Eine breite, von allen getragene Übereinstimmung innerhalb einer Gruppe.
de.usage_note: Anders als eine Einigung wird er nicht ausgehandelt, sondern stellt sich als geteilte Haltung ein.
examples: In diesem Punkt sind sich inzwischen fast alle Fachleute einig.

en.definition: Broad agreement shared by everyone in a group.
examples: There is now a broad consensus among experts on this point.

it.definition: Un ampio accordo condiviso da tutti all'interno di un gruppo.
examples: Su questo punto c'è ormai un ampio consenso tra gli esperti.

notes: Reasoning-error family, next to concept-c1-schlussfolgerung. This branch models Trugschluss as a conclusion that seems valid but rests on flawed reasoning.

de.definition: Eine Folgerung, die richtig wirkt, aber auf einem Denkfehler beruht.
examples: Aus zwei zufällig gleichzeitigen Ereignissen eine Ursache abzuleiten ist ein klassischer Denkfehler.

en.definition: A conclusion that seems valid but rests on flawed reasoning.
examples: Inferring a cause from mere coincidence is a classic fallacy.

it.definition: Una conclusione che sembra valida ma si fonda su un errore di ragionamento.
examples: Dedurre una causa da una semplice coincidenza è una fallacia classica.
